textByHashes.892d123cfa03d1eecb89d94f388eb9322dfd2959: Good day, Bwana. The Kharazi jungle is especially beautiful today, isn't it?
textByHashes.e388fc855545cd12e34fe385516ea0a7c85d24d3: A temple? There's an old dungeon in the south east corner of the jungle. Could this be what you seek, Bwana?
textByHashes.cc7325c2d8cea1b7343d9044bf3794fa7227aa02: I must warn you, Bwana, that place is dangous. Many of our people have entered and never returned.
textByHashes.51d06db93231975c06a4fa6c9cf354a3909e33f0: Did you try that dungeon in the south east corner of the jungle, Bwana?
textByHashes.913c2d163a96d43505b13a9d12a6a79291345b00: Greetings, Bwana. Why do you make such strange sounds and disturb the peace of the jungle?
textByHashes.275c6d272acf290069f82ed040b9939da907801b: Well, it worked! I am Gujuo, proud member of the Kharazi tribe. What did you want to talk about, Bwana?
textByHashes.6b1e1ac38efe1835e68d25a9f431c4cf03b5bfa6: Very good, Bwana... this is indeed a friendly gesture. However, you've come at a bad time.
textByHashes.331cddf1f16a564023caf2df27fd4f16188d897e: My people are scattered and in hiding.
textByHashes.858440932635b02f6a56048a0c99b3d4f0601703: We normally meet regularly around our tribe's totem pole. Unfortunately, ours has been polluted by an evil spirit, and now we are all afraid to approach it.
textByHashes.2940abb209820f9ce3d0da2e9406986c8aaf1c53: We send our shaman, Ungadulu, to confront the spirit... but he has not returned.
textByHashes.2da3c6a73dda91b6585aeb4e8b438286438e75ed: We could work together to make a new totem pole - but I'm afraid it won't be easy. We carve them from the trunk of the sacred Yommi tree.
textByHashes.f08487e89b97fd5b4ec64ce8e17abff4816df0ba: First we need to plant a sacred Yommi tree. It is a magical tree of great power. Our shaman, Ungadulu, is the only one trusted with the seeds.
textByHashes.b4793307b77ab07ce2280e442194dbb6045cfa2c: And as I said, he hasn't returned from his mission. He was supposed to be communing with the spirits in some caves in the northwestern part of the jungle.
textByHashes.05d140d66f8786e2ebd585964ee56f825575cadf: You mean it? That would be wonderful, Bwana! He was last seen in the northwestern part of the Kharazi Jungle, near the great cliffs. Look there, and you will see three rocks that form a triangle.
textByHashes.b96ff21104687ae301e12da43da64d98ec0b343c: They mark the secret entrance to the caves where Ungadulu communes with the spirits. If you rescue him, he will surely entrust you with the Yommi tree seeds, and we can restore our totem.
textByHashes.402090ad3a3918feb46ddbc5a879807a57b926b6: I have to collect herbs now, Bwana...
textByHashes.df80add3802deb9a95d83258cd3253ea70cdbef8: That's okay, Bwana. Ungadulu has probably been torn apart by wild beasts, or driven mad by spirits... there's no point searching for him now.
textByHashes.0095936491a9ed6abba288208d047971d2aaefe3: If my people survive this calamity, it will be a miracle...
textByHashes.ddd7c74055472bac79f6491a5c0a897ee6ca707b: Well, the Kharazi would be forever in your debt. Return our shaman, and we will certainly be able to establish friendly relations with your Legends' Guild.
textByHashes.50e8ac2e8cfb1e8e9bc3652bb2a882a06262fcfd: I can take you to the edge of the Kharazi Jungle. Shall we go now?
textByHashes.ccc7536d8ccfc960dbcf83bdd0d4982dabad8796: As you wish... Again, Bwana, What is it that brings you to the Kharazi Jungle?
textByHashes.1190a92c138771a35bca308ff4e193c4ab268062: That's quite alright, Bwana. But I have to ask... what are you doing in the Kharazi Jungle?
textByHashes.e76eafaad6bb28b5bbdc887b3b2762393a60acbd: Well, they were still there last time I checked... go west through the jungle until you reach the see, then walk eastwards again with the barrier rocks to the north.
textByHashes.701219ca4b2e9880d86e1b6190ec9d3ce95591d7: Walk past the covered pass and you'll find three rocks, arranged in a triangle shape on slightly elevated ground. You'll find the caves there.
textByHashes.59bc37f07d7768e1f3e23f69d88efbcab37ecbf9: I am tired, Bwana, I must go and rest...
textByHashes.44110196bca1d1ac0df6210e2eedaacc854d30b8: Be wary, Bwana. The jungle is full of strange and powerful spirits. You may have been tricked...
textByHashes.afbe6fca4f9efe0dc1681c6cd4027159397e0667: Our forefathers summoned many strange spirits, in an attempt to learn their wisdom.
textByHashes.340315c853ee1ee41faba1ab10f608cc6a34fb64: But something went wrong. Now they haunt the caves around this area, and make our lives miserable. Only the magic of the totem keeps them at bay.
textByHashes.d241db79931462802545d732cfaccc5afa5c3935: These spirits are full of tricks. Take nothing as it first appears.
textByHashes.8ce823a99db5de8ac60da5caaa35c3ed5610b027: That's fine Bwana, not to worry. I hope your quest is going well.
textByHashes.d095e02a655c89d19b99fe7416354186ece9a077: Such matters are best left to the shamans. I am told that certain patterns, scored on the ground, can bind a spirit and prevent it from doing harm... but that's all I know.
textByHashes.4fe47b69f29b0f85eaa04347db57044593c17c04: I wish I could help, but I'm as confused as you. Normally I'd call upon Ungadulu to solve a mystery like this... but he's part of the puzzle!
textByHashes.6f53f1b02e129c64da7c62969cc790ee1cf251c1: This all sounds very odd. You're not possessed, are you Bwana?
textByHashes.c87290724516547eec66a5f33cf13998b57214ab: Well, then I suppose there's no harm in telling you. There is a sacred pool, right at the centre of the Kharazi jungle.
textByHashes.7b9378c071b83b1fbdebfe3849f4aaab46690102: The water contains special properties, but they will only be retained if it's stored in a blessed vessel made from the metal of the sun. It's no easy task, but I'm sure an adventurer like you could manage it.
textByHashes.54f1893f507021e57d5d3eb584e065a40e2ed415: It is a bright and precious metal, much sought after, but uncommonly found. It is the same bright colour as the sun, and yet its light never fades. A blessed vessel made of this metal protects the purity of the water.
textByHashes.984a24cd20268074eda7bdf3936cd43a74d5ba4e: But be careful. This metal can influence people in strange ways. Fall under its power, and you will think of nothing else for the rest of your days, no matter how much of it you find.
textByHashes.fb01d806fcf1f838e90e53efb2a6f712b53bfa51: That's why we only use it to create blessed vessels for the sacred water.
textByHashes.eae25610bdd76ccfb1a1a5f49a3eb35809f7ba77: A bowl would hold plenty of water. However, it must be made from the metal of the sun, and it must be blessed.
textByHashes.f670acfeefacba31eb5e2544f21e1a9a32bb2ce5: Here, this is what the vessel should look like... I hope that helps.
textByHashes.9e1c7f0f45ab2306f56652047d41f60efcfb901a: Similar to the one in the picture I have already given you.
textByHashes.a3cc74ebfcd6bbb8de8596178e7ff91df80267c3: When you have made the vessel, bring it to me. I will help but you need to ensure that you are devout and have faith. Your Prayer will be thoroughly tested.
textByHashes.2d6c3df2775e1235d04054aff682e2e43b0a7d2a: It seems that Ungadulu has started to lose his senses. In the days of our forefathers, the 'Vacu' were shamans who could summon powerful, wise spirits.
textByHashes.f3cd340ce909db87e8bf323cd1f2430406a3dc74: But they asked too much, and were enslaved by the spirits they sought to command.
textByHashes.065168971197ce2f6e50b0cef86f519ded09ece2: Indeed. Every Kharazi child knows not to mess with evil spirits. What has our shaman gotten himself into?
textByHashes.88e3e0a4d55af5e793091ef3a9a891aae3548e2c: I have work to do Bwana, best of luck on your quest...
textByHashes.817371db3b253a0cbc884f02677873007c526108: Greetings, Bwana. Ah, I see you have the golden bowl! Would you like me to bless it for you?
textByHashes.9c9942b0e85c612331786119d44afee186fb10da: Bwana... your faith is not strong enough to bless this vessel.
textByHashes.724c5e5104e3ba914fcd673c7b5f4e005f98287f: Well done, Bwana! This is a fine vessel for the sacred water. Shall I show you how to bless it?
textByHashes.afbdbed0cb5be71e34c98d1b3401222189471ec2: Very well, let me know when you want to try.
textByHashes.f4c52eaaecd05110c53bff4ee3d874045f8976e5: Sorry, but I don't need that, Bwana.
textByHashes.ae4777bf03baa86446b2acb97ca68a447b1d77be: How goes your quest to release Ungadulu, Bwana?
textByHashes.9db3bddbdf238104af438a9182923740fd27b489: That sounds odd. Perhaps our shaman has lost his mind. My apologies, Bwana, it seems that I've sent you on a fool's errand. Unless...
textByHashes.45727170c726b5594a49af380bc7b77ff8596c86: We do have a sacred pool which contains pure water. I don't know why Ungadulu would need it, though. Maybe you should talk to him again and get more information.
textByHashes.f929f83b8bc18fb3945c0a742f8e83a7387a6c71: Farewell, Bwana, and good luck on your quest.
textByHashes.5f19c0324c7fe6d0f50db3bdae42662782374770: Hmm... we have a pool of pure, sacred water nearby. I wonder if that's what Ungadulu meant?
textByHashes.01a12e00490310d3435bf6fdd762c9b397b0bf6e: I am sorry, Bwana. Without the totem pole, it isn't safe to gather together. The evil spirits could strike at any time...
textByHashes.859302817320746b527fc7f0841785e861fef926: If Ungadulu has requested your help, I suppose I can tell you. It's in the centre of the Kharazi jungle.
textByHashes.cccb55d9c07aea0e79b734e474173385977a6d75: But be careful! The water contains special properties which will only be retained if it's stored in a blessed vessel made from the metal of the sun.
textByHashes.03165c9b2d9a88d3c6c4eb283a474d8689fc2381: Don't you need to take some pure water down to the caves? If you haven't got the water yet, try getting some from the pool.
textByHashes.b30f9fa18c9f6160a12354d859e0c3fb3c29a4c7: I am sorry Bwana, but I have no adventuring experience to speak of. I would only get in your way...
textByHashes.b3f05959d18acb2d0fbb7fbd05dc6b404a2b8f19: How goes your quest to free Ungadulu?
textByHashes.8c88a17dcd6d8cc732183f371b792cf92e2b4a6f: A demon? You are truly brave, Bwana. Well done, and thank you for freeing our shaman.
textByHashes.59032b6bb014e2c12122720fb1950ca755a664d4: That's great, Bwana. Now you just need to germinate the seeds and then plant them in some fertile soil. I'm sure that Ungadulu has explained all this to you already.
textByHashes.dd4dd6bd204103e21b160453c22244c728133d37: If you have the Yommi tree seeds, you will need to germinate them.
textByHashes.6122214229b6b38f6f48f5e450387334acaa9652: Place the seeds into pure water, and they will begin to sprout tiny shoots. You can then plant them in fertile soil.
textByHashes.3ef58c50f21dd30946a3eceea6ad9be68ad50ae1: Thank you for releasing Ungadulu! My fellow Kharazi and I feel much safer now that our shaman is back. Is the Yommi tree growing well?
textByHashes.7aafb18aab858c10e6dceaf07fd437e4d7b5b92f: Well done, Bwana. Thanks to your hard work we'll soon have a new totem pole. Now you need to plant the seed in the fertile earth.
textByHashes.3730e05475a621aca14d55c7248f2bfadc32c066: There's lots of fertile ground in the Kharazi jungle. My people have marked the patches we've seen with old wood.
textByHashes.103776737bf0470671703166c3cb3ea63ffbf629: If you find a rotten tree, or what looks like a rotten totem pole, it's probably on fertile soil.
textByHashes.6006541f976c18a5f1ca0236cd3e96fb0f517c60: You'll need to chop the markers down with a sharp Woodcutting axe.
textByHashes.7a3d6f363df804f0a87ce5f66f3f0d8b4911f5a2: I have visited Ungadulu in the caves. He's hard at work studying, but he looks well! Have you grown the Yommi tree yet?
textByHashes.4f3c63cdfc923f0854a17dac2119abee28c5e028: This is indeed a bad omen, Bwana. I've never known the sacred pool to dry up before...
textByHashes.34d1336be8dec3e5433cf6b303246bd77559821a: Of course! It is a magical tree and can only survive on the water from the sacred pool.
textByHashes.f5775b33cf7a29775c6444bab5bf5062bd76e2cc: Oh, Bwana, it is a terrible place. Be thankful you don't have to go there.
textByHashes.36b6053ff983b771bd0595631d0ef2604fd21d7b: Bwana, you cannot be thinking of travelling to the source!
textByHashes.d35992c96e12784286f8c65751c31a8e26a211cb: But I suppose we have no choice...
textByHashes.448f2804193563ff88ca4fa6b9861036b1932249: Deep in the caves where you found Ungadulu is a terrible place, guarded by the angry spirits of the undead.
textByHashes.dd01fd7af634011b7de9f1ba38a05977ae0e846a: The legends say they died trying to find the source of the sacred water, and now they are cursed to guard it for all eternity.
textByHashes.22e6673ddd314d77d8d378752e702eef8aaa0fd2: The first to die was a powerful sorcerer, and his strange magic lingers in the cave. Now all who venture near are struck by terror, and cannot proceed.
textByHashes.24669df2e8472770f8a7a6e43d40104cc12f9d9a: With all my heart, Bwana, nothing could convince me to go near such a place.
textByHashes.0755dba1fbd8b787b442edf632df053cc9dd7a28: That's sensible of you, Bwana. It would be a waste of a perfectly fine life. We will just have to restore the totem some other way... or else leave our home forever...
textByHashes.d07e6643230de79604283cdabdb71bb6b4f06676: If you're determined to find the source, I will help as much as I can. First, you must overcome the curse of fear that blocks the cave.
textByHashes.fe8a9f71f99bd756089891c73abe67da923d864e: I know a potion that may help with that.
textByHashes.d37a02505d273a2a3b1c48f12eb659805e6058cb: You will need to find two herbs, Snake Weed and Ardrigal. Add them both to a Vial of Water, and you will feel braver than a lion!
textByHashes.54682f9390d01296b9cdc449e6885a683ecba10a: Snake Weed is usually found in swampy, marshy areas.
textByHashes.d9fc593b824d8fc97f5f08fb0cfdefd4a9de3574: There's some marsh to the south of Tai Bwo Wannai village - maybe you could check there? It likes to grow near jungle vines, so look carefully.
textByHashes.0646a651c94bc019536acd78e3ce5ff0887e5fb9: Ardrigal is often found growing near large groups of palms. If you head east out of Tai Bwo Wannai village you should come across a big group of them.
textByHashes.a1bbfc8f37cf8ee8bb08fb3d81e8c2215f8a000f: The herb grows in the shade of the palm, so check carefully.
textByHashes.ec0559e5c4914c951b5f3d7029399ddc4980a3e9: The caves are protected by a supernatural curse which would leave even the bravest adventurer a trembling wreck.
textByHashes.99f62e86641db92dabd494642a98fb42f09f1540: You must take the potion if you plan to journey there.
textByHashes.1c13f6ab38825b623a0387eacd5d1b5bc18a99b7: The source of the sacred water is a terribly dangerous place. Perhaps the elders have hidden the path, or buried it under rubble?
textByHashes.e732b64a539cb7f0598d6d29466c3de9abccfe60: Well, it requires pure, sacred water for it to grow. It is a very special tree...
textByHashes.112180b21297c9a6237a1492bd3b3c78f01fff55: I have visited Ungadulu in the caves. He's hard at work studying, but he looks well! How is your quest going, Bwana?
textByHashes.bb2e95e2c636c388cf3b03441847ba2033eade46: If the pool of sacred water has dried up, there may be a way to get to the source of the spring. But it is said to be very, very dangerous.
textByHashes.c23b96b014beac0a303cc2bcb20f95c7faf0122f: That's great, Bwana! But please, be careful...
textByHashes.69bb596e8902d5cac95933fa95472d22ed9f4958: I have told you everything I know, Bwana.
textByHashes.43c5683e018bd15016fc3d76c62c2cbf35797f55: It seems familiar. Maybe I heard it from the Elders...?
textByHashes.aa4516cc43d644da90706f48f104afb28cf577e7: Of course! Viyeldi was the name of the sorcerer who first went in search of the sacred water's source.
textByHashes.335d55907cc22a257553e2d33c32d3c124d60e47: Have you encountered his spirit? Be careful, Bwana... he must be very powerful.
textByHashes.73cb4a32154d2f1c4a516c77ac1158f707b24544: Ouch! The mosquitoes are biting! I'm going for a swim...
textByHashes.4984b4b4857ee1f3ff34ec84c4538b7bcfaee665: Deeper in the caves where you found Ungadulu is a terrible place, guarded by the angry spirits of the undead.
textByHashes.289235c8d5013c8e3a107622cb5abbf7830126ba: The path must be well hidden. You should take another look around the cave where you found Ungadulu. The entrance is most certainly there somewhere!
textByHashes.71d9b2e00ce8b398824fd42a674555ad7962355b: Bwana! You're alive! I'm pleased to see you.
textByHashes.c38825eaa9646397d2c5b6a1af17ba89ea31dce0: Something feels different about the jungle... I suspect it's down to your good deeds.
textByHashes.410cabcc7fc509d063a997145b08e9dc2fd8f895: You are truly a brave warrior, Bwana. Now your Yommi tree can grow to be just as strong as you are - and we'll have a new totem pole in no time.
textByHashes.1e5f0a135b3b7d65310d070fbbdd555868b29b62: Can it be true? My people and I have noticed a difference in the jungle. The trees seem to sing again, the air tastes fresh and cool... and we have you to thank.
textByHashes.b8151b58a810b1e8bd720fa254ddd4f787e3b953: First, your Yommi tree must be fully grown. Then, you must chop it down - quickly, before it rots!
textByHashes.e9d9d0eeb565c1538b79fb228e46fe956a75edcf: Once you have felled the tree, trim the branches.
textByHashes.f5fe142a431126b53cc17c94dfb05c54b0b9894a: Finally, you can craft a new totem pole from the bare trunk.
textByHashes.c463597bec3c05460d7435e8926a11370becbf82: You'll need a very sharp, very tough axe for all of this.
textByHashes.ae5b69d871f3caed95898233d0eeae88553dbaa9: But when you're done, you can replace one of the rotten totem poles, and the Kharazi will be forever in your debt!
textByHashes.74ba020c19e24ad7f3becb884713949c0400f24b: I must check in on my people... these are dangerous times.
textByHashes.ab7df8c87b484f4b3c2158efa01f3fb8c3f80ce7: Well Bwana, that's a very interesting piece of work! Not bad at all.
textByHashes.e370abd4ef3f42558afc0b0cade07558c825f4dc: Now we need to replace an existing corrupted totem pole with your newly constructed one. You should be able to find totems within the Kharazi Jungle.
textByHashes.ea62e197880008f16c219e3958cd2b63f69aba71: Greetings, Bwana! I can tell you've already placed the totem. The jungle feels alive again, and my people can finally gather without being bothered by evil spirits.
textByHashes.8979f5993442e9d102aeb6c0d7ce3cbd747c0eab: You have done us a great service, Bwana.
textByHashes.29f791716097e3b44019a503509a30f3dc24d433: Please accept this token of our friendship.
textByHashes.664f35dc1ea1020d9e594016ad8afa00359237fe: Take this to your Legends' Guild - let them know that you are a friend of the Kharazi. And please, come back and visit us anytime.
textByHashes.94a3e9591b1034228f34e8f65f85aa9290a25f1d: I must go and hunt now, Bwana...
textByHashes.6e21a3dae78ed7d705d880e65093d6fc23a75743: None at all! Everything is fine now, thanks to you.
textByHashes.b0813fb8df244e9009a994259182ced5be7ffb9d: The evil spirits you vanquished have haunted this jungle for many years. It will take some time for my people to fully trust strangers - even strangers as famous as you!
textByHashes.43805fe0370308b59a8c9e071aa959ab219d791b: Rest assured, you will always be a friend of the Kharazi.
textByHashes.4545fae61b6afeaddc9e5b9ff62441b9e057729e: Lost it? That totem pole was a sign of our friendship, Bwana...
textByHashes.6dfcfc872e6c56c6bc0d4d6b1526c2762477ca54: Luckily, I made an extra one. Please be more careful with it this time.
